title: MIG Gun Nozzle and Contact Tip Inspection Cleaning
summary: This section covers routine maintenance procedures for the Vulcan OmniPro 220, including pre-use inspections, periodic interior cleaning, and storage. It provides detailed steps for inspecting and cleaning the MIG Gun Nozzle and Contact Tip, as well as replacing the LCD Screen Cover.
key_facts: Inspect the general condition of the Welder before each use.
A qualified technician should periodically blow out interior dust with compressed air.
Store the Welder in a clean and dry location after every use.
Clean and inspect the MIG Gun Contact Tip and Nozzle before each use.
The Contact Tip hole should be an even circle, not oblong or bulged.
Replace the Contact Tip if any problems are noted, selecting the correct size for the wire used.
The Nozzle end should be flat and even; replace if uneven, chipped, melted, or cracked.
Clean the inside of the Contact Tip with a tip cleaner sold separately.
Check that the Contact Tip is the proper type for the wire size used.
Insert the new LCD Screen Cover into the Screen Frame with the gap facing downward.
Reinstall the Screen Frame into the Display slot with the top end placed in first.

procedure_steps.procedure: MIG Gun Nozzle and Contact Tip Inspection and Cleaning
steps: 1. Make sure the entire MIG Gun is completely cool and the Power Cord is unplugged from the electrical outlet before proceeding.
2. Pull the Nozzle to remove it.
3. Scrub the interior of the Nozzle clean with a wire brush.
4. Examine the end of the Nozzle. If the end is uneven, chipped, melted, cracked, or otherwise damaged, replace the Nozzle.
5. Unscrew the Contact Tip counterclockwise and slide it off the welding wire to remove.
6. Scrub the outside of the Tip clean with a wire brush. Clean out the inside of the tip with a tip cleaner (sold separately). Check that the Tip is the proper type for the wire size used.
7. Examine the shape of the hole at the end of the Contact Tip. It should be an even circle; it should not be oblong or have any bulges in it.
8. If any problems are noted, replace the Contact Tip. Select a new Tip of the correct size for the welding wire used.
9. Reinstall the Tip and securely reinstall the Nozzle as well.
1. Carefully pry the frame off of the Display by inserting a flathead screwdriver (sold separately) into one of the side slots.
2. Insert a new Screen Cover into the Screen Frame with the gap facing downward.
3. Insert the frame back into the slot on the Display with the top end being placed in first.

warnings: TO PREVENT SERIOUS INJURY, FIRE AND BURNS: Unplug the Welder, rest the tool on a heat-proof, electrically non-conductive surface, and allow all parts of the Welder to cool thoroughly before service.
